strategic_objectives: raise cost through proxies and deniable operations
threaten oil transit without inviting total isolation
protect strategic program continuity and elite cohesion
keep multiple retaliatory pathways available even after strikes

constraints: elite cohesion risk under major damage
readiness_drivers: air-defense persistence around the strategic core
ability to sustain oil exports or reroute them

decision_doctrine.risk_tolerance: high for indirect escalation, lower for openly attributable regime-endangering war
decision_doctrine.escalation_bias: incremental and layered, using proxies and maritime pressure before full direct exposure
decision_doctrine.negotiation_posture: accepts talks that preserve deterrence and regime prestige
red_lines: major damage to the Tehran-Qom strategic core
severe hits on export capacity or command continuity
perceived collapse of proxy deterrence credibility
off_ramps: restored export flow and strategic-site survivability
proxy pause that does not appear coerced
reduced US force concentration near the Gulf littoral

economic_pressure_points: damage to strategic energy and industrial sites
loss of maritime confidence in the Gulf and Gulf of Oman
currency and inflation stress that feeds legitimacy risk
intelligence_priorities: how Gulf states calibrate infrastructure defense and mediation

priority_fronts.name: Hormuz Littoral
priority_fronts.goal: retain the ability to threaten traffic without triggering uncontrollable backlash

priority_fronts.name: Western Proxy Corridor
priority_fronts.goal: keep Iraq-Syria-Lebanon pathways usable for deterrence and signaling

priority_fronts.name: Strategic Core
priority_fronts.goal: protect command continuity and strategic-site survivability

priority_fronts.name: Khuzestan and Export Belt
priority_fronts.goal: preserve revenue, domestic confidence, and energy leverage

strategic_relationships.notes: Important for maintaining the sense of distributed retaliation.

strategic_relationships.dependency: pressure on US presence and corridor depth
strategic_relationships.notes: Useful for deniable escalation but politically variable.

strategic_relationships.dependency: mutual signaling to avoid regime-threatening misread
strategic_relationships.notes: Iran closely watches carrier posture, base hardening, and sanction rhetoric.

strategic_relationships.dependency: deterrence through uncertainty and proxy depth
strategic_relationships.notes: Iran treats Israeli operational freedom as a major indicator of vulnerability.

strategic_relationships.dependency: their hedging behavior affects both isolation and mediation space
strategic_relationships.notes: They are simultaneously infrastructure targets, economic comparators, and de-escalation intermediaries.

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Strait of Hormuz
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: threatenable on demand but not fully uncontrollable

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Bandar Abbas to Gulf of Oman outlet
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: usable for layered maritime signaling and export resilience

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Iraq-Syria-Lebanon corridor
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: porous enough for proxy support and messaging continuity

geopolitical_chokepoints.name: Khuzestan export belt
geopolitical_chokepoints.desired_state: kept functioning enough to sustain revenue and legitimacy
